subject: Human Resources
title: Culture Fit vs. Culture Add
description: One-minute skill: Culture Fit vs. Culture Add.

hook.text: A hiring manager rejects a qualified candidate because they 'wouldn't fit.' Translation: they don't look or think like us.

buildup.text: Culture fit asks: does this person match our existing culture? It values similarity, comfort, and shared norms.

discovery.text: Culture add asks: what unique perspective, experience, or skill does this person bring that we're missing?

twist.text: Culture fit is the top reason for homogeneous workplaces—people hire people like themselves and call it 'alignment.'

climax.text: Shifting from fit to add—valuing what someone brings that's different—creates diverse, innovative, and resilient organizations.

punchline.text: Don't hire people who fit in. Hire people who make you better.

quiz.question: What is the difference between culture fit and culture add?
options: Fit values similarity; add values unique perspectives that are missing
Culture add means hiring unqualified people
